issue.title: Replace large inline PHP adapter raw block

artifacts.role: project rule forbidding large inline raw public adapter bodies without structured IR or provenance-tracked compiler templates
WPHX PHP emission manifest adapter_templates collection and deterministic recording
WordPress-profile adapter template renderer and historical WP_Http request nonblocking template use
runner requiring adapter template provenance before accepting WPHX-312.65 generated shell
candidate manifest recording adapter template shape checks
public-shell snapshots regenerated with adapter_templates manifest field

claims: WPHX PHP manifests now record compiler-owned adapter template provenance.
The bounded WP_Http::request nonblocking public adapter body no longer lives as a large inline Haxe string concatenation.
This checkpoint has been superseded by structured WPHX PHP IR emission for the same request nonblocking boundary.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership or whole-file WP_Http ownership.
This does not claim that adapter templates are runtime implementation authority or generated target files.
This does not claim WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
